memoryArtifacts.headline: Will of Robert Melton of Orange County, North Carolina
memoryArtifacts.description: In an effort to preserve old records, many years after this 1759 document was made a typescript copy was typed (probably  in the 1930's) on an old style typewriter and was filed in the SECRETARY OF STATE RECORDS, NORTH CAROLINA STATE ARCHIVES. This typescript can be viewed on microfilm at the Family History Library, Salt Lake City, Utah, US/CANADA film # 18,048, Pages 2656-2657. The transcription displayed here with paragraph spacing and names in bold print was done to enable quick referencing.
memoryArtifacts.place: Orange, North Carolina, British Colonial America

memoryArtifacts.headline: Robert Melton Sons in ABSTRACT OF NORTH CAROLINA WILLS, (1759)
memoryArtifacts.description: Short summary of Robert Melton 1759 Will. Sons listed are: James, Nathaniel, Nathan, Isesum, and Ancel. Youngest son Archelius (sic) is left out of this abstract but is named in the actual Will.
memoryArtifacts.place: Orange, North Carolina, British Colonial America

memoryArtifacts.headline: 1759 Will of Robert Melton (old 1930's typescript from the original), Orange County, North Carolina, British Colonial America
memoryArtifacts.description: Will, dated April 24, 1759, for Robert Melton of Orange County, North Carolina was proved September Court 1759.  Will names Robert Melton's six sons:  James Melton, Nathaniel Melton, Nathan Melton, Isesum Melton, Ancel Melton, and Archelus Melton.  Son, Nathan Melton named "Sole Executor."  Subscribers identified as Richard Parker, James Sellars, and Mary Stratten. Document appears here as a pdf, so it has two pages, so be sure to scroll down to the second page.
memoryArtifacts.place: Orange, North Carolina, British Colonial America

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Melton於1696年5月17日出生在美國維吉尼亞州劍橋大學默里 肯特聖 比特也斯 帕里什時，他的父親Richard Melton26歲，母親Mary Ross21歲。他於大約1730年與Mary Elizabeth Farrar在美國維吉尼亞州劍橋大學默里 肯特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1759年4月24日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加北卡羅來納州奧拉享年62歲，埋葬在美國北卡羅來納州奧拉查阿培爾 希爾。

nameMeanings.name: Melton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Yorkshire, all of which have the same origin as Middleton , with Old English middel replaced by its Old Norse equivalent methal after the Scandinavian settlement of northern and eastern England.

variants.name: Middleton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places so called. In over thirty instances from many different areas, the name is from Old English midel ‘middle’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. However, Middleton on the Hill near Leominster in Herefordshire appears in Domesday Book as Miceltune, the first element clearly being Old English micel ‘large’, ‘great’ (see Middlebrook ). Middleton Baggot and Middleton Priors in Shropshire have early spellings that suggest gem̄thhyll (from gem̄th ‘confluence’ + hyll ‘hill’) + tūn as the origin. This surname is also found in Scotland and Ireland.

History: A Scottish family of this name derives it from lands at Middleto(u)n near Kincardine. The Scottish physician Peter Middleton practiced in New York City after 1752 and was one of the founders of the medical school at King's College (now Columbia University) in 1767. One of the earliest of the Charleston, SC, Middleton family of prominent legislators was Arthur Middleton, born in Charleston in 1681. The name has also been established in Ireland since at least the 13th century; its presence was reinforced by settlers in the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: The Tuscarora War
historicEvents.description: The British, Dutch, and German settlers fought with the Tuscarora Native Americans, from September 22, 1711- February 11, 1715, in North Carolina.
North Carolina is the location of Cape Hatteras, sometimes called the Graveyard of the Atlantic- many shipwrecks have occurred nearby.

historicEvents.title: Becomes a Royal Colony
historicEvents.description: July 25, 1729, North Carolina became a royal colony, when the colony was sold to King George II.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Anchor-Carolina Becomes North and South Carolina

historicEvents.title: Mount Vernon
historicEvents.description: Mount Vernon Plantation was the home of George Washington. It started off as 2,000 acres and was later expanded to 8,000 acres. The house itself started off as a six room building then got extended to twenty-one rooms.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Melton was born on 17 May 1696, in St. Peter's Parish, New Kent, Virginia, United States, his father, Richard Melton, was 26 and his mother, Mary Ross, was 21. He married Mary Elizabeth Farrar about 1730, in New Kent, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 24 April 1759, in Orange, North Carolina, British Colonial America, at the age of 62, and was buried in Chapel Hill, Orange, North Carolina, United States.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Melton(이)가 1696년 5월 17일 미국 버지니아 주 뉴 켄트 스트. 피터'스 파리시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Melton(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Ross(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1730년 미국 버지니아 주 뉴 켄트에서 Mary Elizabeth Farrar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1759년 4월 24일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 노스캐롤라이나 주 오랑에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 오랑 차플 힐에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Melton nasceu em 17 de maio de 1696, em St. Peter's Parish, New Kent, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Richard Melton, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Mary Ross, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Elizabeth Farrar aproximadamente 1730, em New Kent, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de abril de 1759, em Orange, Carolina do Norte, British Colonial America, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Chapel Hill, Orange, Carolina do Norte, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Melton родился 17 мая 1696 г. в местности ст. питир'с пэриш, ню кэнт, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Richard Melton было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Ross, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Elizabeth Farrar около 1730 г. в местности ню кэнт, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 24 апреля 1759 г. в местности оранг, Северная Каролина, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности чапел хилл, оранг, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты.
